title: A Novel About a Secret No One Wants to Know
description: The wildly popular Norwegian novelist Vigdis Hjorth returns to a dark past.
hook: A Norwegian novelist excavates a family secret no one wants unearthed—psychological depth meets dark revelation.

title: “Wuthering Heights” Was Never a Love Story
description:
“This is a strange book,” begins a January 8, 1848 review of Emily Brontë’s Wuthering Heights. “It is not without evidences of considerable power: but, as a whole, it is wild, confused, disjointed, and improbable . . . ” Another review, published a week later, drew similar conclusions: “Wuthering Heights is a strange sort of […]

The post “Wuthering Heights” Was Never a Love Story appeared first
hook: Reframing Wuthering Heights as something other than romance reveals Brontë's savage examination of power and cruelty.

title: A Sojourn into the Stephen King Archive: ‘The Dark Half’
description: "Typescript drafts on view in the newly opened archive reframe the horror maestro’s relationship with his alter ego, Richard Bachman."
hook: Stephen King's archive reveals his creative wrestling with Richard Bachman—the psychology of artistic alter egos laid bare.

title: The Last Kings of Hollywood by Paul Fischer review – the rise and reign of Spielberg, Lucas and Coppola
description:
An epic account of how three demigod directors, in pursuit of indie freedom, redefined American film-making

Here we are once more: back to the glory days of the New Hollywood that emerged from the ashes of the old studio system in the 1960s and 70s. Our cast is filled with brilliant hotshots and creative risk-takers, energised by the French New Wave, the American counterculture and the industry’s own amazing entrepreneurial past.

Peter Biskind’s breezy, bleary, cynical book Easy R
hook: How Spielberg, Lucas, and Coppola redefined American cinema by chasing indie freedom—ambition and reinvention across decades.

title: Being a Soulmate Is Something You Choose
description:
Kelsey L. Smoot’s debut full-length poetry collection, SOULMATE AS A VERB, is a necessary addition to a long lineage of works beckoning us toward love and liberation. It invites us to examine who or what can be a soulmate and wonders what the world would look like if we were soulmates to not only our […]

The post Being a Soulmate Is Something You Choose appeared first on
hook: Poetry collection redefines soulmate as verb and choice—contemplative work on love, liberation, and collective belonging.
